page: Microsoft Azure D15_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 20 physical cores and 20 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 140.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1024 GB of local storage. The CPU architecture is x86_64, featuring a 50 MB L3 cache and supporting AVX, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 and AMX capabilities.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench tests, which is also reflected in weak single-threaded text processing. However, the 20 dedicated physical cores elevate multi-core performance to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores. Memory read bandwidth is average for both small and large blocks, though large-block write bandwidth is weak.

This server is suited for workloads that benefit from multi-threaded execution and a moderate memory footprint, such as database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, which all scored in the average tier. It also supports small to medium LLM inference tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D15_v2 offers a high density of physical cores and a substantial 1024 GB local storage allocation, making it a viable option for legacy enterprise applications and database hosting where single-core speed is not the primary bottleneck.
description: The Microsoft Azure D15_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. It features 20 physical cores and 20 vCPUs running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 140.0 GB of RAM and 1024 GB of local storage. While single-core benchmarks from Geekbench and stress-ng place its performance in the bottom tier, its multi-core capabilities, memory read bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. The server supports AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets but lacks AVX-512. With 7.0 GB of memory per core and a bundled 1024 GB storage drive, the D15_v2 is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, database hosting, static web serving, and software compilation. It offers a balanced resource profile for enterprise applications requiring dedicated physical cores without needing top-tier single-thread performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D15_v2 is a Dv2-family VM featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 140 GB RAM, and 1024 GB local storage, delivering average multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D15_v2 is a 20-core Intel Xeon VM with 140 GB RAM and 1024 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded database workloads.
tagline: A dedicated twenty-core Intel Xeon virtual machine featuring balanced memory capacity and a one-terabyte local storage allocation for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 20 physical cores
140.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core
Bundled 1024 GB of local storage capacity
Average multi-core performance across CPU and database benchmarks
Suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
